understanding.project_summary:
Confirmed: AutoDocX is a FastAPI application that scans a local Python project, parses and analyzes its Python source, stores scan results, builds compact project contexts for LLM consumption, and asks configured LLM providers (OpenAI or Anthropic) to generate a structured "project understanding". The API exposes endpoints to scan a project, list and retrieve saved scans, compare scans, build project context, and request a generated understanding. Key components visible in the code: app.api.project_routes (HTTP handlers), app.services.project_scanner (scanning), app.services.code_parser (parsing Python files), app.services.project_analyzer (project-level analysis), app.services.scan_storage (persistence), app.services.scan_comparator (diffing), app.services.project_context_builder (context generation), and app.services.project_understanding_service + app.services.llm.* (LLM integration).

Reasonable inferences: The runtime is synchronous (no async functions in API handlers or services), configuration (LLM provider/model/key and workspace paths) is loaded via app.core.config and environment variables, stored scans are JSON files under workspace/scans/<project_name>/<scan_id>.json, and the LLM providers are selected through LLMProviderFactory which normalizes provider names and instantiates OpenAIProvider or AnthropicProvider. There may be a workspace created at startup by create_required_directories in app.core.config. Some behavior (concurrency, request timeouts, size limits) is not visible in the supplied context and is therefore uncertain.
understanding.architecture_overview:
Confirmed: The application is organized as a FastAPI service (app/main.py) which mounts an APIRouter defined in app.api.project_routes. The router delegates work to service classes in app.services: ProjectScanner (scans filesystem and delegates file parsing to PythonCodeParser and analysis to ProjectAnalyzer), ScanStorage (persists and retrieves stored scan JSON), ScanComparator (compares two stored scans), ProjectContextBuilder (converts stored scan into either detailed or LLM mode context), and ProjectUnderstandingService (builds LLM-ready context and uses an LLM provider to generate structured JSON). LLM integrations follow a provider pattern: app.services.llm.base defines BaseLLMProvider; OpenAIProvider and AnthropicProvider implement it; LLMProviderFactory selects and instantiates providers. app.models.project contains Pydantic models used as request/response schemas. app.core.config provides configuration helpers including get_llm_provider, get_llm_api_key, and get_llm_model.

Reasonable inferences: Typical request flow is: API handler -> relevant service(s) -> storage/LLM provider -> response. All API handlers are synchronous and likely run inside the FastAPI worker process. Stored scans are placed under a workspace directory (ScanStorage docstring). The prompt builder (app.services.llm.prompts) assembles user/system prompts used by providers. The project is intended to run locally and operate on local filesystem paths supplied to the scan endpoint. Some runtime details (how environment variables are loaded, any background tasks, or rate limiting) are not visible and therefore uncertain.

execution_flow.title: Application startup and routing
execution_flow.description: app/main.py creates a FastAPI app and mounts router from app.api.project_routes as project routes. app.core.config.create_required_directories may run at startup to ensure workspace directories exist (inferred).

execution_flow.title: Health check
execution_flow.description: A simple GET /health (handler health_check in app.main) verifies the service is running.

execution_flow.title: Scan a project
execution_flow.description: A client POSTs to /api/projects/scan (scan_project in app.api.project_routes). The handler constructs or calls into ProjectScanner.scan_project which lists files, computes file hashes and sizes, calls PythonCodeParser.parse_file for Python files, and then calls ProjectAnalyzer.analyze_project to build project-level relationships. The final scan result is returned and may be saved using ScanStorage.save_scan.

execution_flow.title: Store and list scans
execution_flow.description: ScanStorage.save_scan persists scans into the workspace (documented save path). Clients can list saved scans via GET /api/projects/{project_name}/scans and fetch specific or latest scans via GET /api/projects/{project_name}/scans/{scan_id} or /latest.

execution_flow.title: Compare two saved scans
execution_flow.description: POST /api/projects/{project_name}/compare calls ScanComparator.compare_scans with two stored scans. The comparator reports added/removed/modified files, symbols, routes, and internal dependencies.

execution_flow.title: Build project context
execution_flow.description: POST /api/projects/{project_name}/context calls ProjectContextBuilder.build_context with a stored_scan and mode ('detailed' or 'llm'). The builder returns a structured context optimized either for retaining full scan detail or for LLM consumption.

execution_flow.title: Generate project understanding via LLM
execution_flow.description: POST /api/projects/{project_name}/understand triggers ProjectUnderstandingService.generate_understanding. It uses ProjectContextBuilder to build an LLM context, builds prompts via app.services.llm.prompts, obtains provider selection and credentials (get_llm_provider, get_llm_api_key, get_llm_model from app.core.config or from request), instantiates a provider via LLMProviderFactory.create_provider, and calls provider.generate_json to obtain structured JSON. The provider implementations (OpenAIProvider, AnthropicProvider) call respective SDKs and parse returned text into JSON via BaseLLMProvider.parse_json_response.

module_responsibilities.responsibility: Confirmed: Defines HTTP routes for project scanning, listing/retrieving scans, comparing scans, building contexts, and requesting LLM-based project understanding. Handlers orchestrate calls to ProjectScanner, ProjectContextBuilder, ProjectUnderstandingService, ScanComparator, and ScanStorage. Reasonable inference: handlers also obtain LLM config via app.core.config helpers when generating understanding.
Confirmed: Application entry point that creates the FastAPI app, registers routes (router alias project_router), and defines basic health endpoints (root, health_check).

Confirmed: Provides configuration helpers and environment bootstrapping (load_dotenv referenced). Exposes get_llm_provider, get_llm_api_key, get_llm_model, and create_required_directories. Reasonable inference: these functions read environment variables and are used by API handlers and services to select LLM provider and credentials and to ensure workspace directories exist.

Confirmed: Pydantic request/response models for the API. Defines model classes used as request and response schemas (scan requests/responses, context/understanding requests/responses, comparison models, scan storage metadata).

Confirmed: PythonCodeParser reads a Python file and extracts structural information: imports, functions, async functions, classes, class methods, FastAPI routes, content hashes, and syntax errors. parse_file returns a dictionary describing the parsed file.

Confirmed: ProjectScanner orchestrates filesystem scanning: listing files, ignoring unnecessary files/folders, computing project stats and SHA-256 file hashes, parsing Python files with PythonCodeParser, and invoking ProjectAnalyzer. Exposes scan_project to produce a complete scan dictionary.

Confirmed: ProjectAnalyzer.analyze_project consumes parsed file dictionaries and constructs project-level relationships (module mapping, internal dependencies, functions/classes/methods, FastAPI routes and include_router resolution) and project statistics.

Confirmed: ScanStorage persists and retrieves scan JSON files under the workspace. Methods include save_scan, get_scan, get_latest_scan, and list_scans. The docstring documents storage path workspace/scans/<project_name>/<scan_id>.json.
Confirmed: ScanComparator.compare_scans compares two stored scans and reports diffs: added/deleted/modified files, functions, classes, methods, routes, and internal dependencies.

Confirmed: ProjectContextBuilder.build_context converts a stored scan into structured project context; supports modes 'detailed' (keeps full metadata) and 'llm' (produces compact context for LLM input).

Confirmed: Generates LLM-based structured project understanding. Uses ProjectContextBuilder to create the LLM input, constructs prompts via app.services.llm.prompts, selects provider via LLMProviderFactory, and invokes provider.generate_json to obtain and return parsed JSON.

Confirmed: BaseLLMProvider is the abstract interface for LLM providers. It requires provider_name and generate_json, and provides a static parse_json_response helper to extract JSON from provider responses (supports code fences and surrounding explanatory text).

Confirmed: LLMProviderFactory.create_provider and normalize_provider_name select and instantiate a concrete provider (AnthropicProvider or OpenAIProvider) given provider name, api_key and optional model.
Confirmed: OpenAIProvider implements BaseLLMProvider using OpenAI Responses API (OpenAI SDK imported). It implements provider_name and generate_json to communicate with OpenAI and return parsed JSON.

Confirmed: AnthropicProvider implements BaseLLMProvider using Anthropic SDK (Anthropic import) and a Messages-style API. Implements provider_name and generate_json to call the provider and return parsed JSON.

Confirmed: Provides prompt construction helpers for LLM requests. build_project_understanding_prompt turns a project_context dict into a user prompt, and a system prompt constant PROJECT_UNDERSTANDING_SYSTEM_PROMPT is referenced by ProjectUnderstandingService.

api_overview.purpose: Health / root endpoint defined in app.main; returns a simple JSON to indicate service availability.
Health endpoint defined in app.main; used to verify service is running.
Scan, analyze, and (likely) save a local project. Request/response models: ProjectScanRequest -> ProjectScanResponse. Implementation delegates to ProjectScanner and ScanStorage.

Compare two saved project scans. Uses ScanComparator to report added/removed/modified files, symbols, routes, and internal dependencies. Request/response models: ScanComparisonRequest -> ScanComparisonResponse.
Build compact LLM-ready project context from a stored scan. Request/response models: ProjectContextRequest -> ProjectContextResponse. Delegates to ProjectContextBuilder.

Generate structured project understanding using the configured LLM. Request/response models: ProjectUnderstandingRequest -> ProjectUnderstandingResponse. Delegates to ProjectUnderstandingService and the LLM provider layer.
List all saved scans for a project. Returns list[ScanSummaryResponse]. Delegates to ScanStorage.list_scans.
Return the latest stored scan for a project. Returns StoredScanResponse. Delegates to ScanStorage.get_latest_scan.
Return a specific stored scan by id. Returns StoredScanResponse. Delegates to ScanStorage.get_scan.

key_dependencies.purpose: scan_project handler delegates project scanning to ProjectScanner
compare_project_scans handler delegates comparison logic to ScanComparator
understand_project handler uses ProjectUnderstandingService to generate LLM-based understanding
ProjectScanner calls PythonCodeParser.parse_file to extract structure from Python files
After parsing files, ProjectScanner delegates project-level relationship building to ProjectAnalyzer
ProjectUnderstandingService builds LLM-ready context via ProjectContextBuilder
ProjectUnderstandingService creates a concrete LLM provider using LLMProviderFactory
Factory may instantiate OpenAIProvider when normalized provider name maps to OpenAI
Factory may instantiate AnthropicProvider when normalized provider name maps to Anthropic
Handlers refer to get_llm_provider/get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model for provider configuration and credentials

risks_and_gaps.title: Synchronous/blocking LLM and IO calls
risks_and_gaps.description: All API handlers and service methods in the provided context are synchronous. Long-running operations (scanning large repositories, calling remote LLM APIs) may block worker threads and degrade throughput. No visible background task queue or async calls to mitigate long requests.

risks_and_gaps.title: LLM credential and configuration handling
risks_and_gaps.description: Credentials and provider selection are accessed via app.core.config helpers, but the exact secret handling, rotation, and validation are not shown. There is risk of misconfiguration, accidental logging of secrets, or lack of support for multiple credentials per request.

risks_and_gaps.title: Error handling and timeouts for remote providers
risks_and_gaps.description: Provider implementations have generate_json methods, but the context does not show retry policies, timeouts, rate-limit handling, or graceful fallback logic when the provider fails or returns malformed output.

risks_and_gaps.title: Parsing and JSON extraction reliability
risks_and_gaps.description: BaseLLMProvider.parse_json_response attempts to extract JSON from free-form LLM responses (code fences, surrounding text). This is brittle and may fail for malformed or unexpected responses; the robustness of parsing and validation of returned structured data is unclear.

risks_and_gaps.title: Storage consistency and concurrency
risks_and_gaps.description: ScanStorage writes JSON files under workspace/scans/<project>/<scan_id>.json but file locking, atomic writes, or concurrent access policies are not visible. Concurrent saves or reads could cause partial reads or race conditions.

risks_and_gaps.title: Limits on project size and memory usage
risks_and_gaps.description: Behavior for scanning very large projects (memory usage of parsed structures, size limits for LLM inputs, chunking) is not shown. ProjectContextBuilder has an 'llm' compact mode, but any enforced size trimming or chunking strategy for LLM prompts is not visible.

risks_and_gaps.title: Test coverage and operational tooling
risks_and_gaps.description: No tests, CI configuration, or runtime monitoring/logging code are visible in the provided context. Operational aspects (metrics, structured logs, tracing) appear missing from scanned files.

risks_and_gaps.title: Input validation and request quotas
risks_and_gaps.description: While Pydantic models exist, it's unclear how large or malicious inputs are limited for endpoints that accept local filesystem paths or externally-provided scan payloads. No rate-limiting or authentication/authorization appears in the provided modules.

recommended_document_sections.title: Getting Started
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Step-by-step instructions to set up the environment, install dependencies, populate environment variables (LLM keys), run create_required_directories, and start the FastAPI server using provided scripts.

recommended_document_sections.title: API Reference
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Document each endpoint (paths, methods, request/response schemas from app.models.project), examples of requests and responses, and common error responses.

recommended_document_sections.title: Architecture and Component Diagram
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Describe high-level components (API, Scanner, Parser, Analyzer, Storage, Comparator, ContextBuilder, LLM providers) and interactions, including sequence diagrams for common flows (scan, store, build context, understand).

recommended_document_sections.title: LLM Integration and Provider Guide
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Explain provider selection, supported providers (OpenAI, Anthropic), how LLMProviderFactory works, how to supply api_key/model/provider via configuration, and guidance on prompt construction (app.services.llm.prompts) and response parsing.

recommended_document_sections.title: Storage and Workspace Layout
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Document workspace path conventions, scan file layout (workspace/scans/<project_name>/<scan_id>.json), file naming, retention policy recommendations, and backup/cleanup guidance.

recommended_document_sections.title: Security and Secrets Management
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Guidance on securely managing LLM API keys, recommended environment variable practices, avoiding secret leakage in logs, and authentication/authorization considerations for the HTTP API.

recommended_document_sections.title: Operational Concerns and Scaling
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Notes on synchronous blocking behavior, suggested async or background task patterns, recommended request timeouts, retry policies for LLM calls, monitoring/metrics, and scaling approaches for large projects.

recommended_document_sections.title: Testing and Validation
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Recommended unit/integration test targets (parsing, analysis, comparator, storage, provider mocking), sample test cases, and instructions to run tests.

recommended_document_sections.title: Error Handling and Troubleshooting
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Common failure modes (parsing errors, provider errors, storage IO issues), log locations/formats, how to reproduce issues locally, and recommended remedies.
